cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The user wants to know what Steve Rowson thinks about newer football helmets. The options are A, B, C, D. First, I need to go back to the passage and find where Steve Rowson is mentioned. The passage says: "Steve Rowson is an engineer at Virginia Tech. In his lab, he tests how helmets hold up against different types of hits. No helmet can prevent concussions completely, says Rowson. But he's found that when wearing a newer football helmet, the player's head doesn't gain speed as much after a hit. That lowers the risk of concussions." So, Rowson's main point is that newer helmets reduce the speed gain of the head after a hit, which in turn lowers concussion risk. Now, looking at the options: A) They vary a lot in size. The passage doesn't mention size variation, so probably not. B) They work better than the old ones. The passage states that newer helmets make the head not gain speed as much, which implies they are more effective than older ones. This seems correct. C) They slow down the player's running speed.
